Title: Sabah claim used as political tactic in Philippines, says Hajiji
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hjiji Noor said the Sabah claim is often turned into a polemic by certain parties in the Philippines, particularly as a political tactic during election seasons.
He said that Sabah's status as part of the Malaysian Federation is final and has been recognised by the United Nations (UN), leaving no room for further dispute.
"Over there, these issues tend to surface close to their senate elections. But the people of Sabah have long decided to be part of Malaysia. This is not up for debate.
"We are a sovereign state within Malaysia, recognised by the UN. So what else can they say?" he told reporters after attending the state-level Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house at the Sabah International Convention Centre here today.
He added that any attempt to revive such claims would only create confusion and could harm bilateral relations between the two nations.
Hajiji urged all parties to respect Malaysia's sovereignty and territorial integrity and refrain from using the issue for political gain.
Recently, media reports noted that Philippine senator
Robin Padilla
had urged President Ferdinand Marcos Jr to reactivate the country's claim over Sabah, citing potential oil and gas resources in the state.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heatwave dries up Sungai Pahang in Chenor, exposes part of riverbed
Article:
MARAN: Peninsular Malaysia's longest river, Sungai Pahang, appears to be experiencing one of its worst dry spells in recent years, with water levels rapidly declining.
Certain sections of the river in Kampung Pesagi, Chenor, near here have almost completely dried up, leaving behind a narrow water channel along the banks and exposing large stretches of the riverbed.
The dried-up riverbed at the traditional settlement now allows villagers to walk to an island located in the middle of the river, which is covered with trees and was previously accessible only by boat.
Caged fish breeders operating along the river have been forced to sell their
ikan patin
(silver catfish) in bulk or risk suffering losses as the fish become stranded in the increasingly shallow waters.
Villager Setafar Chek, 67, whose house is located less than 70m from the river, said the water had risen rapidly during the rainy season in December last year and nearly overflowed onto the road in front of his house.
"Now, the water has completely receded, leaving the riverbed exposed. It rained heavily last month during Chinese New Year, but since then, rainfall has been minimal.
"This is nothing unusual for villagers, as we have faced similar situations over the years. Previously, the hot weather would begin in late April, but this time it started earlier," he said when contacted.
Born and raised in Kampung Pesagi, Setafar said the last rainfall in the area occurred more than two weeks ago and lasted only a short while.
He said Sungai Pahang in the village has not completely dried up, but only one section facing Kampung Pesagi is affected, while water continues to flow on the other side of the island.
"Currently, more than 1km of the riverbed is exposed, and there are concerns that more areas could dry up if the hot weather persists.
"The river began showing signs of drying up during hot weather over the past five to six years, and the situation has worsened over time," he said.
Setafar said that the drop in water levels has forced fish farmers to sell their stock at bargain prices over the past few weeks or risk further losses if the fish die.
Meanwhile, in Jerantut, the extreme heat over the past few weeks has led to the formation of sandbars measuring about 100m wide and 200m long in Sungai Tembeling, disrupting daily life, especially for those who rely on the river for transportation.
Kampung Mat Daling villager Nurul Natasha Abdul Aziz, 25, described the phenomenon as seasonal, saying that prolonged dry weather alters the river's course, causing boats to run aground more easily due to shallow water levels.
Last year, the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, said Sungai Pahang was very shallow and called for the deepening, and widening of rivers across the state.
The ongoing hot and dry weather phenomenon has been cited as a
major contributor
to the widespread forest and plantation fires across Pahang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 40pct rise in construction costs may push up house prices
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A potential 40 per cent spike in construction costs, fuelled by prolonged conflict in the Middle East, is expected to drive up property prices as developers struggle to mitigate rising material costs.
Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) Shah Alam Faculty of Built Environment's senior lecturer Dr Muhammad Azwan Sulaiman said that when input costs rise, developers are faced with two choices, either absorb the costs, which would affect profit margins, or pass a portion of them on to buyers through higher selling prices.
The cost of building materials, such as cement, steel and sand, accounts for a significant portion of a project's cost structure.
He noted that, technically, "in the current reality, most developers are unable to absorb the entirety of the cost increases; therefore, to some extent, this will translate into a rise in house prices.
"However, the impact is not uniform.
"Projects in the affordable housing category may be more strictly regulated through government policies, whereas commercial or 'high-end' projects are more likely to experience price hikes," he told Harian Metro.
He said these cost increases also place significant pressure on developers in terms of overall operating costs, including logistics, labour and financing.
Azwan said the ongoing instability in global supply chains — characterised by shipping disruptions, geopolitical conflicts and raw material price fluctuations — has further heightened uncertainty for developers.
He said this situation makes it difficult for developers to provide accurate cost estimates during the early stages of a project.
"This condition can lead to cost overruns, construction delays and negatively impact cash flow for developers," he said.
Under certain circumstances, developers may postpone or restructure the launch of housing projects, he said.
"This situation also affects investor and buyer confidence, as prices and project completion timelines become less certain," he said.
He said an integrated approach must be taken to manage the impact of rising costs and ensure house prices remain affordable.
He suggested that developers could optimise project designs, adopt technologies such as the Industrialised Building System (IBS), and strengthen material procurement strategies.
"The government can also play a role by providing targeted incentives, implementing price controls on critical materials in specific situations, and expediting project approval processes.
"Long-term measures, such as strengthening the local supply chain, are also vital to ensuring that house prices remain stable and affordable," he said.
It was reported that the national construction industry is expected to face building material cost increases of between five and 15 per cent at a minimum.
However, the figures could soar to 30 or 40 per cent if conflicts in the Middle East persist.
Mohd Hawarie Tan Muzi, chairman of the Malaysian Housing Contractors Association in Perak and a central committee member, said these projections depend on the duration of the conflict, global supply chain disruptions, and rising fuel costs that continue to strain construction operating expenses.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heatwave splits tourism fortunes in Penang as visitors adapt to extreme temperatures
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The ongoing heatwave sweeping across the country has delivered mixed fortunes for tourism operators, with some reporting slower business due to extreme temperatures while others continue to enjoy steady — and in some cases increased — visitor numbers.
Beach and water sports operators said the scorching weather has discouraged outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours, although mornings and evenings remain busy.
Jet ski operator Khairul Izdihar Ismail, 36, who has been in the business since 2015, said the past few days had been extremely hot, resulting in fewer customers during the day.
"There were hardly any customers yesterday afternoon. Now it is slow in the afternoons, but mornings and evenings are still okay," he told the New Straits Times when met at the Batu Ferringhi beach here today.
Khairul said they are now mainly depending on domestic tourists for business.
[[nid:1405293]]
Concurring with Khairul is Muhammad Salman Mohd Amin, 36, who said that demand has dropped compared to peak periods.
He said during school holidays and weekends, he would typically record between 20 and 30 trips daily.
However, current bookings have struggled to even reach 10.
Besides the heatwave, Salman also attributed the situation to the serious erosion happening along Batu Ferringhi beach, which is pushing away tourists.
"It is a bit slow now. I believe the heatwave had slightly impacted our daily income.
"Business has declined by more than 50 per cent," he lamented.
To make matters worse, he said the increase in petrol prices also did not help their business.
Despite the slowdown, he said prices for activities such as jet ski rides, parasailing, banana boat and other water sports have remained unchanged.
"If we raise prices, people won't ride," he added.
However, not all destinations have seen a decline.
In urban areas, shopping malls have become a preferred refuge, with visitors seeking relief from the heat instead of outdoor attractions.
At Penang Hill, visitor numbers have remained strong, benefiting from its cooler climate.
Penang Hill Corporation general manager Datuk Cheok Lay Leng said the hill has been busy since the Hari Raya period, recording an increase in visitors compared to last year.
"For the past seven days from Raya eve until March 27, we have seen more than a 13 per cent increase compared to the same period in 2025," he said.
He added that during the first four days of the Hari Raya holidays from March 20 to 23, visitor numbers rose by 18 per cent year-on-year.
"The temperature on the hill is about four to five degrees Celsius lower than at the foothill area, with even bigger differences in the early morning and evening.
"So, visitors may be taking the opportunity to enjoy the cooler temperature up on the hill," he told the NST.
[[nid:1405292]]
For holidaymakers Nur Syasya Subri, 20, and Siti Nur Atikah, 31, from Alor Star, Kedah, the current heatwave has made people "think twice" before heading outdoors.
Syasya said indoor venues are becoming more attractive as temperatures rise.
"The temperature these few days is much hotter than it used to be," she said.
Similarly, Siti Nur Atikah, 31, said she preferred to remain in the shopping malls during the day to enjoy the air conditioning.
Tourists from cooler climates, meanwhile, appeared less affected by the heat.
[[nid:1405290]]
Jackie Hole, 60, a nurse from the United Kingdom, said she was enjoying her stay in the beach hotel here despite the weather.
"It is not an issue. We came here to get away from the cold," she said when approached.
Another tourist, Richard Hyatt, 60, also from the UK, described the weather as "hotter than usual" but said it would not deter their travel plans.
"In fact, we are heading to the beach now," he told the NST when met about 2pm.
As Malaysia continues to grapple with the heatwave, tourism operators are adapting to shifting visitor patterns, balancing quieter afternoons with busier mornings and evenings, while hoping for more favourable weather conditions in the coming weeks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno supreme council to discuss Hishammuddin, Khairy's applications on April 17
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The Umno supreme council will discuss the applications of several former leaders to rejoin the party on April 17.
This includes the applications of Sembrong member of parliament Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin,
Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the discussion would be held based on the spirit of "Rumah Bangsa", but the decision on the applications would not necessarily be announced at the meeting.
"I will raise this matter at the meeting in the spirit of Rumah Bangsa, as well as the spirit of Aidilfitri and Ramadan. The party president's statement also views this matter positively, and we hope for a good decision," he told reporters after officiating at the JanaVeteran Project in Telok Sengat here today.
He said they are still collecting applications, as no deadline has been set for former leaders or members who wish to rejoin the party.
Among those who have applied are Hishammuddin, Shahril Sufian Hamdan, Datuk Seri Mohd Salim Sharif and Datuk Maulizan Bujang, whose memberships were suspended for six years.
Former Selangor Umno liaison committee chairman Tan Sri Noh Omar and Khairy, meanwhile, were sacked for offences committed during the 15th General Election (GE15).
The suspensions and sackings were made at the Umno supreme council meeting on Jan 27, 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Targeted diesel subsidy for East Malaysia in the works, says Armizan
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The government is currently studying a framework for targeted diesel subsidies in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan before initiating further discussions with the respective state governments.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali dismissed claims that the federal government was being unfair by maintaining bulk diesel subsidies in the three regions for now.
He clarified that the government has never decided against implementing targeted diesel subsidies there, explaining that the rollout is being done in stages.
"The government's policy from the start has been to reform subsidies through a targeted approach to ensure aid reaches eligible groups and sectors accurately.
"Subsidy targeting is a major reform that requires meticulous planning and phased implementation, he said after the Sabah state government Aidilfitri reception at the Sabah International Convention Centre today.
Armizan noted that while targeted diesel subsidies began in the peninsula in 2024, the nationwide targeted RON95 petrol subsidy was implemented in 2025.
Fundamentally, diesel subsidies will continue but through targeted methods to curb leakages, including misappropriation and smuggling, he said.
Armizan said the delay in implementing targeted subsidies in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan was to account for local factors, such as high diesel dependency, geography, and the significant price gap for basic goods.
High logistics costs for shipping goods from the peninsula also played a key role in the current retention of bulk subsidies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lawyer seeks High Court review of jail sentence for couple over indecent act at cemetery
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A lawyer has filed an appeal on behalf of a couple who were sentenced to one year's jail by the magistrate's court here on Thursday after pleading guilty to jointly committing an act of gross indecency at a Chinese cemetery.
Datuk Naran Singh filed the appeal at the High Court yesterday.
Speaking to the New Straits Times, Naran said he believed there had been a miscarriage of justice.
"I want the High Court to review the sentence imposed on both accused, to determine whether it is manifestly excessive in the circumstances of this case.
"As an advocate and solicitor and officer of the court, I can do that (file the appeal)," said Naran who submitted the appeal online.
He said he would follow the necessary procedures and request an early hearing date.
On Thursday, the magistrate's court sentenced M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, to
one year's jail
after they admitted to the charge under Section 377D of the Penal Code.
Magistrate Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi handed down the sentence after taking into account the facts of the case and public interest.
Both admitted to jointly committing an act of gross indecency at a Chinese cemetery in Batu Gantung, Ayer Itam, at 8.30am on March 22.
The charge was framed under Section 377D of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same Code, which provides for punishment under the same provision.
The provision criminalises acts of "gross indecency" between persons, whether in public or private, and carries a penalty of up to two years' imprisonment.
Police began investigations after receiving a report from a 69-year-old man regarding a
video
showing a man and a woman behaving inappropriately at the cemetery.
The woman was arrested at 8.35pm on Monday at a flat near Jalan Padang Tembak.
The man was detained at 11.10pm in front of a building near Jalan Paya Terubong in Sungai Ara, Bayan Lepas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yeoh: Residents Representative Council has no right to check licences, talk to business owners
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Residents Representative Council (MPP) has no right to check licences or talk to business owners if it received complaints, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh.
Following a viral video showing two MPP members speaking to businesses and a subsequent police investigation into alleged extortion linked to the matter, Yeoh said all enforcement matters fall under the jurisdiction of Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL).
"They (MPP) coordinate issues and complaints. They are not enforcement officers.
"The MPP's role is to listen to grievances and channel those complaints to DBKL.
"Therefore, there is no reason for MPP members to go to the ground to conduct checks, inspect licences or engage in 'discussions', as that falls under the jurisdiction of Kuala Lumpur's enforcement authority, DBKL," she said after the Decathlon Malaysia Kiprun launch here today.
Yeoh said MPP also had no right to meet developers, as there were proper channels for those discussions, such as forums and town halls.
She vowed to eradicate middlemen practices in Kuala Lumpur and said all dealings must be made directly with DBKL.
"While we are currently in the process of making it easier to do business in Kuala Lumpur, there are still limits and regulations.
"To apply for licenses and such, deal directly with DBKL. Do not go through anyone acting as an agent or middleman. I want to abolish the practice of using middlemen in Kuala Lumpur."
She said she would speak to members of parliament before deciding on the next steps following the controversy involving MPP.
"I will listen to the MPs on this matter because they are the elected representatives of the people.
"It is vital that the MPP truly represents the voices of residents in their respective areas."
Previously, Yeoh urged the public to report any misconduct to the integrity units of the Federal Territories Department, DBKL and Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), for cases involving not only MPP, but also any officer or agency under the supervision of the Federal Territories Department.
Complaints can be lodged with the Federal Territories Department at www.jwp.gov.my/a/2060, MACC at portaladuan.sprm.gov.my and DBKL at adukl.dbkl.gov.my.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Middle East conflict: Increase in MSME fuel costs may reach 50pct
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Fuel costs among micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in the country are at risk of soaring by up to 50 per cent of total operating costs if the conflict in Middle East continues.
SME Corporation Malaysia chief executive officer Rizal Nainy said the increase could occur following the rise in global oil prices, which have now surpassed US$100 per barrel and are expected to continue to rise if the conflicts do not ease.
He said the situation would affect the global supply chain, thus directly impacting the import and export activities of MSMEs, especially sectors that rely on raw materials such as textiles, cement and steel.
"According to a survey by SME Corp, fuel costs currently only contribute around 6.4 per cent of the total operating costs of MSMEs.
"However, if the crisis is prolonged, the percentage may increase by up to 50 per cent and put great pressure on business continuity," he said after the 2026 Hari Raya Muhibbah Ceremony at the Elmina Rainforest Knowledge Centre here today.
Also present at the ceremony was the Selangor State Youth, Sports and Entrepreneurship exco member Mohd Najwan Halimi.
Meanwhile, Rizal said that although the food and beverage sector has yet to feel a significant impact, cost pressures are expected to increase in the medium term.
He said MSMEs are urged to immediately diversify export markets to reduce dependence on high-risk regions such as Middle East.
He said logistic strategies need to be adapted, including the use of alternative routes even if they involve higher shipping costs.
"SME Corp also provides guidance on free trade agreements involving 16 agreements to open up new market opportunities because of the 1.1 million MSMEs in the country, with 15 to 16 per cent involved in international trade and are expected to be affected by the increase in costs.
"MSMEs are also urged to improve operational efficiency through digitalisation, in addition to preparing contingency plans to deal with the current economic uncertainties," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia cannot unilaterally withdraw Malbatt from Lebanon, says Khaled
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia cannot unilaterally withdraw its Malbatt 850-13 peacekeepers from Lebanon as the deployment is subject to the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mandate.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the mission is under the United Nations (UN), including command and any decision on troop withdrawal.
"We cannot suddenly decide to return; otherwise, the Malaysian military's professional standing will be questioned. We must follow the proper process if such action is taken.
"However, what is important, I believe, is that we will suspend all roles and responsibilities if necessary. In other words, if the crisis continues, if they have to 'stay in the bunker, then stay,' and do not come out," he said at a press conference following the launch of the JanaVeteran project at Ladang Telok Sengat, here.
Khaled also said that Malbatt personnel had encounters with the Israel Defence Forces (IDF), but the situation remained under control with no untoward incidents.
"There have indeed been attacks, and there were also incidents where they encountered them (IDF personnel), (it was) not a clash, just an encounter, the IDF did not take any action. This is a newly occurred incident.
"However, we are grateful that nothing serious happened. I have already instructed that this must not happen again. Therefore, we have asked that they monitor each other and maintain proper conduct in Lebanon.
"We are peacekeeping forces; we do not carry weapons to attack, and that is part of the rules. We did not expect this to happen. Hence, the most important priority for us is the safety of our personnel," he said.
On March 25, Khaled said no Malbatt personnel were injured in several explosion incidents reported near the Unifil operational area in southern Lebanon.
On March 13, Malbatt peacekeepers escaped injury after an air strike by the IDF hit as close as 100 metres from their position after they targeted a bridge crossing the Litani River.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: More CCTV around KLCC to crack down on photo touts, says minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: More closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras will be installed around the area of the Petronas Twin Towers (KLCC) in Jalan Ampang here, in the near future to combat the 'photo touts' syndicate.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said her office will hold further discussions with KLCC Property Holdings Bhd and the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to increase the number of CCTV cameras in areas that attract local and foreign tourists.
"CCTVs are under the KLCC management. I will discuss with them, along with the DBKL, to see how we can install more CCTVs outside so that we can identify the faces of those conducting such business activities without permission," she said to reporters here today.
She said this in response to the issue of 18 unlicensed photographers suspected of attacking a man who tried to break up a fight between their group and a foreign tourist at Persiaran KLCC last Sunday.
Yeoh said she also wants police officers and DBKL enforcement officers to patrol more frequently to prevent the issue from continuing.
"We do not want to see tourists and Malaysians being bullied because it also tarnishes the country's name.
"Fights like that should not happen and an investigation is being conducted by the police. I urge the police to take action," she said.
Yesterday, the Dang Wangi district police chief ACP Sazalee Adam announced was reported as saying that
18 unlicensed photographers
, aged between 17 and 45, were detained in the vicinity of the KLCC for further investigation under Section 148 of the Penal Code.
Meanwhile, Yeoh said DBKL has filed a police report regarding the issue of Federal Territory Residents' Representative Council (MPP) members who allegedly asked for money from a massage centre, believed to have occurred in Wangsa Maju.
Yeoh, who is also the Segambut member of parliament, said that the council members should play a role in conveying residents' complaints to DBKL.
"There is no reason for MPP members to go down to the field because that is the jurisdiction of DBKL.
"The MPP listens to complaints and directs them to the DBKL. MPP members are not enforcers. That is clear," she also said.
ecently, a CCTV recording from a massage centre went viral, showing two men introducing themselves as MPP members before
soliciting protect
ion money to prevent the premises from being closed, allegedly due to complaints from residents.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Seven Malaysian tankers awaiting 'safe window' to transit Strait of Hormuz
Article:
REMBAU: Seven Malaysian-owned tankers are currently awaiting the signal to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened tensions in the Middle East.
Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the vessels include three owned by Petronas, two by MISC Bhd, and one each by Sapura Energy and Shapadu Corporation Sdn Bhd.
He clarified that the ships are not being detained; rather, they are waiting for a safe window and an official signal to proceed.
"The Malaysian government has held preliminary discussions with Iran, which provided a positive response to our request.
"I personally negotiated with my counterpart, followed by a call from Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to the Iranian president to strengthen our application.
"Iran has stated there is no issue in allowing our ships through the Strait of Hormuz. For now, cargo ships have been released, while tankers are required to wait a little longer," he told reporters at his Aidilfitri open house in Kampung Tanjong Rantau today.
Mohamad, who is also the Rembau member of parliament, noted that communication between the command centre in Tehran and field commanders is occasionally disrupted by the conflict, leading to temporary delays.
"We have requested passage, and Iran said they are waiting for a 'window'. Sometimes communication there is difficult; Tehran may say yes, but the commanders on-site must fully understand to prevent any untoward incidents," he said.
It was previously reported that seven Malaysian vessels, four of which are laden with crude oil, were stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.
National Union of Seafarers Peninsular Malaysia (NUSPM) executive secretary Ikmal Azam Thanaraj Abdullah had said the remaining two are a pipe-laying vessel and an offshore support ship.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said communication with Malaysians in Iran is currently limited due to system disruptions, but the ministry is monitoring the situation through alternative channels.
On the issue of Malaysians planning to perform the umrah, he said they may proceed as international airspace remains open, noting that recent strikes have targeted strategic locations related to Israel.
"However, if they can postpone their travel, they are advised to do so, given the current uncertainty. But if bookings have already been made, it is up to them as flights are currently unaffected," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PKR denies Nurul Izzah resignation rumours
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Claims that Nurul Izzah Anwar has resigned as PKR deputy president are "fake news".
PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil dismissed the rumours when contacted by the New Straits Times.
"Fake news," he said briefly.
Speculation over the former Permatang Pauh member of parliament's alleged resignation was circulating on social media earlier today.
Nurul Izzah, the daughter of PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, was elected deputy president for the 2025-2028 term in last year's party polls.
She defeated incumbent Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli in a straight contest, securing 9,803 votes against his 3,866.
Following the defeat, Rafizi stepped down as economy minister.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Water at Bukit Kwong dam may last until June if dry spell persists
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: The storage capacity of the Bukit Kwong dam here is expected to last until June if the current dry spell continues.
Kelantan Drainage and Irrigation Department (DID) director Dr Siti Fairus Zakaria said projections indicated the dam's water level could drop to 12m within the next three months under prolonged dry conditions.
She said based on past operational records, the Bukit Kwong dam has never reached a critical dry state, with its dead storage level recorded at 11m.
"The irrigation system can still function until the water level reaches 12.19m, which provides some buffer despite the declining trend," she said.
Siti Fairus said monitoring data showed the dam's water level had decreased from 15.7m on March 1 to 15m as of March 27.
"This indicates an average decline of about a metre per month, consistent with the ongoing dry spell affecting the state," she added.
Meanwhile, villagers in Kampung Bukit Kwong expressed concern over the worsening situation and its impact on daily life and agriculture.
[[nid:1405230]]
Resident Khadijah Remit, 62, who has lived in the area since she was 16, said the dam was now visibly drying up.
"It is very dry now and I am worried about the impact on farming. I have five children and we depend on water for our livelihood," she said.
Another villager, Rosli Ahmad, 67, described Kampung Bukit Kwong as facing a critical situation, with even stored water supplies running low.
"The authorities need to take immediate action if the dry spell continues. Over the past month, the water level has clearly receded.
"The dam water is also murky now. We are even worried about using it for bathing as it may cause skin irritation," he said.
Residents said they hoped for rainfall soon to ease the situation, as the prolonged dry spell continues to strain water resources in the area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd
